review_text: The Story of Griselda and Reg is amazing.

Great environment in a remote outback location. The resources at the shop/ restaurant were excellent as was the wallaby feeding show.
Wonderful environment but sadly currently very dry, would love to come back after a good rain. Genuine 4WD area, but easy access for smaller vehicles and lots of tours. Excellent swimming pool
Long drive but great place and scenery

Unfortunately the state of the amenities lets this place down. They need to be updated and fully functional. The camp kitchen, however, has new appliances and is functional. The campground is rocky and that could be improved as well.
Amazing views. Emu wondering around. Pub and restaurant, observatory within walking distance. Hikes and walks can be done from the caravan park. Toilets and showers clean and in good working order.

Absolutely thrilled to have visited here. Polite and welcoming staff. We did the 4wd tour, the cosmos star show,(led by an astrophysicist no less) and enjoyed a Sunday roast, even catering to dairy/gluten free!

At best caravan park a one star rating.Camp kitchen BBQ,s were dirty and rusty,some powered sites were out of order.toilets and
showers were not very clean.probably about the worst park I've stayed in.

Poorly maintained. Most things are run down with leaking taps in the powered sites and some don’t even work. Needs a good make over. While the service provided is good the staff are all for the most part backpackers that know very little and are not so well organised for morning tours etc. No dump point. The sanctuary here however is a scenic wonderland so if you can put up with the run down park, the flies and the dust it really is well worth the visit.
Ok but no washing machine and toilets that flow onto floor when flushed. Showers have hot water but place is very run down.

This is a wilderness sanctuary.
Taken years to rid of feral animals and the drought of 2018, 19 and 20 took its toll.
Road is 130km of gravel stones ground. Average speed of 50 kph gets you there.
Campground has 50 powered sites $40 pn and 50 unpowered sites.
Power is pretty reliable.
Shower and toilets all work and kept clean.
Washing machine $3 a load.
You can take various tours, don't damage your vehicle, not worth it here!
Swimming pool is available for all guests.
Yellow footed wallabies and emus come to have a look at the humans everyday!
Once in a lifetime experience, we did the 8am Ridgetop tour. Glad we didn't drive!
Knowledgeable guides with a sense of humour!
Very good facility with great tours available. Tours can be expensive compared to other places visited.

This park is a dirt patch up and rough road from the resort. Facilities were clean but old.  Very dusty and the flies were there in the millions. Most roads are only accessible by 4wd. Definitely didn't have the bush camping  outlook we had seen in the brochures and online.
What an amazing place,loved it, the other places we visited locally were amazing too,great drives,so interesting.,highly recommend.

Good caravan park on top of the hill but down the back there’s some real pearlers. They have perfect shade, seclusion and wind protection and extend up the valley a long way. Explore and you will find a perfect site with plenty of room. Lots of places to turn your van up the valley.
